food_subgroup: Flatbreads and pan-fried dough — sweet variant

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for tissue prata or roti prata. FOODON_03302050 (flatbread food product) is the closest defensible parent class. Strawberry topping is not captured in ontology.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Unleavened bread) is the closest parent for roti prata / tissue prata. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for this Singapore-Indian flatbread variant. Strawberry topping not captured.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 (Flatbread) is the closest parent. No specific code for tissue prata or roti prata exists in SNOMED CT.

preparation_method: served with strawberry topping or jam
main_ingredients: strawberry jam or fresh strawberry sauce

serving.description: 1 piece (one tissue prata with strawberry topping)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one tissue prata (roughly 180 g including the strawberry jam or sauce topping), typically served as a single large, cone-shaped crispy flatbread at a hawker stall or Indian restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Roti Prata (Plain) — adapted with strawberry jam addition; HPB Singapore Foodo and ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / ICMR-National Institute of Nutrition India
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Tissue prata is a variant of plain roti prata made with a very thin, crispy, cone-shaped dough using more ghee/margarine. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo plain roti prata data (per 100 g) with adjustment for the higher fat content of tissue prata (additional ghee used in preparation) and the addition of strawberry jam topping (~15 g per serving). The strawberry jam contribution is estimated using USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 169640, Strawberry jam). Core macro values reflect a weighted composite. This is an estimated adaptation; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Chapati / Roti (wheat flatbread) GI reference — analogous estimate for tissue prata with strawberry
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tissue prata with strawberry. The GI is estimated at 57 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain wheat flatbread / roti (GI ~52–62 range, mean ~57). The strawberry jam topping adds rapidly available sugars which may marginally elevate GI above plain prata; a value of 57 is used as a conservative mid-range estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 71.1) / 100 ≈ 40.5, where carbohydrate per 180 g serving = 39.5 × 1.8 = 71.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~280 mg per 100 g; ~504 mg per serving) and saturated fat from ghee
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit tissue prata with strawberry to occasional consumption, as one serving contributes approximately 504 mg sodium (about 22% of the 2,300 mg daily limit) and significant saturated fat from ghee, both of which can adversely affect blood pressure and vascular health.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~40.5 per serving) driven by refined wheat flour and strawberry jam sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume tissue prata with strawberry sparingly, as the combination of refined maida flour and strawberry jam produces a high glycemic load of approximately 40.5 per serving, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion control and pairing with a protein or fibre source is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~7.2 g per 100 g; ~13 g per serving) from ghee and trans fat presence
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit tissue prata with strawberry due to its high saturated fat content from ghee (approximately 13 g per serving, exceeding 60% of the recommended daily saturated fat limit) and the presence of trans fats, both of which are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tissue Prata with Strawberry is a popular sweet variant of the classic Singaporean-Indian flatbread known as roti prata or canai. The 'tissue' preparation involves stretching the dough extremely thin — thinner than a standard plain prata — and pan-frying it with generous amounts of ghee or margarine until it becomes a large, paper-thin, golden-brown crispy sheet that is typically shaped into a tall cone or folded into a dramatic presentation. The strawberry variant is finished with a drizzle or dollop of strawberry jam, fresh strawberry sauce, or strawberry-flavoured condensed milk, adding sweetness and a fruity flavour contrast to the rich, buttery, slightly savoury dough. The texture is characteristically shatteringly crisp on the outside with delicate, flaky layers. Tissue prata is a hawker staple in Singapore, commonly found at Indian Muslim (mamak) stalls and Indian restaurants. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense due to the refined wheat flour (maida) base and high ghee content, providing approximately 310 kcal per 100 g. The strawberry topping adds sugar, raising the glycemic load significantly. It is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high glycemic load, saturated fat, and moderate sodium content. It is vegetarian and halal-certified in most hawker contexts.
common_user_queries: Calories in tissue prata with strawberry
What is the difference between plain prata and tissue prata?

confidence_reason: No exact nutrient entry for tissue prata with strawberry exists in HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are adapted from plain roti prata data in HPB Foodo with adjustments for higher ghee content (tissue preparation) and strawberry jam addition estimated from USDA FoodData Central. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat flatbread data. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on HPB Foodo plain roti prata and USDA FoodData Central strawberry jam data. An exact tissue prata with strawberry entry was not found in any approved primary source as of the processing date. 2. Vitamin B6, B12, folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, and selenium are reported as -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo roti prata entry or the adapted ICMR-NIN reference used; they are not measured in the source database for this food category. 3. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat flatbread data; no direct GI measurement for tissue prata with strawberry was found in approved sources. gi_category is 'medium' (GI 56–69). 4. The glycemic load per serving of 40.5 is high and clinically significant for diabetes management. 5. Tissue prata is typically halal at Singapore hawker stalls but consumers should verify with individual vendors. 6. The food_id GD003662 is retained from the field survey seed record. 7. Serving size of 180 g is an estimate based on typical hawker portion sizes for tissue prata in Singapore.

Verified all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Verified ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact/closest_parent/none). Confirmed zero-macro justification not needed as no core macro is zero. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass.
